MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christopher Hersom, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Paul Bossert

RULING:
Defendant Cooper Industries, LLC's motion for summary judgment. No other appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production and, even if it had, there is a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant based on plaintiff's deposition and the documents attached to defendant's General Order interrogatory responses. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Wilson's declaration are sustained. Summary adjudication is granted as to Issue 1 (false representation) and Issue 2 (punitive damages). Plaintiff did not oppose these issues. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Alice Y. Ahn, Esq. of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Schneider Electric USA, Inc. f/k/a Square D Company
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party / opposing party Paul Bossert

RULING:
Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant has sustained its burden of production and plaintiff has presented insufficient evidence to create a triable fact regarding asbestos content of any product attributable to defendant that the plaintiff was exposed to. The objections to Mr. Cohen's declaration are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
